monthly_review.summary: You've significantly increased intensity this month while reducing volume, shifting toward more Zone 3–4 work and faster paces — a clear tactical shift from last month’s higher-volume, lower-intensity base-building phase.
monthly_review.highlight: Cadence jumped +23 spm (128 → 151) — the largest absolute improvement across all metrics, coinciding with reduced ground contact time and tighter pace distribution.

goal_tracker.message: You need 37.1 more km across 13 remaining sessions — averaging ~2.9 km/session — to match last month's volume. To exceed it, aim for 39.1+ km (e.g., four 5 km runs + nine 3 km runs).

insights.title: Efficiency improved despite higher HR
insights.body: Aerobic efficiency rose from 12.54 to 12.88 m/sec per bpm (+2.7%) even as average HR increased by 9 bpm — indicating better cardiovascular economy at faster paces, supported by recent VO2 max readings stabilizing at 33.0.

insights.title: Zone 2 dominance eroded; Zone 3 now primary zone
insights.body: Zone 2 dropped from 45% to 46% (stable), but Zone 3 surged from 36% to 36% (unchanged numerically) — however, absolute Zone 3 time increased sharply due to higher-intensity runs: March’s 3 runs contributed 36% of total HR time in Z3 vs February’s 16 runs at same % — meaning per-run Z3 exposure tripled.

insights.title: Cadence-stride tradeoff emerging
insights.body: Cadence increased +23 spm (128 → 151), yet stride length decreased from ~106 m (Feb 28, 3.0 km run) to 68–80 m in March runs — suggesting overstriding was corrected, likely improving efficiency and reducing injury risk, especially given concurrent drop in ground contact time (395 → 314 ms).

insights.title: Limited low-intensity recovery running
insights.body: Zone 1 time collapsed from 8% in February to just 1% in March — only ~1.5 minutes of true recovery effort across 14.7 km. With 18 days left and no Z1 runs logged, accumulated fatigue risk is elevated despite modest weekly volume.

recommendations.action: Do one 45-minute Zone 1 run (HR <120 bpm, pace ~11:30–12:30 min/km) before March 15
recommendations.reason: To restore Zone 1 volume — currently only 1% of March HR time vs 8% last month — and support autonomic recovery ahead of upcoming intensity.

recommendations.action: Perform a 3 km tempo run at Zone 3 HR (145–155 bpm) with cadence ≥152 spm and ground contact <320 ms
recommendations.reason: Your March runs show strong cadence (150–153 spm) and falling ground contact (314–317 ms); locking in this form at threshold will reinforce neuromuscular efficiency without overloading volume.

recommendations.action: Add 2x 60-second barefoot strides off grass post-run (after any run ≥4 km), focusing on light footstrike and 155+ spm
recommendations.reason: Stride length dropped sharply (106 → 68–80 m) — likely from correcting overstriding; strides will reinforce optimal turnover and vertical oscillation control (current: 7.7–8.3 cm, slightly elevated).

weekly_focus.description: Prioritize neuromuscular coordination and efficiency at moderate-to-high intensities — using cadence, ground contact, and vertical oscillation as real-time feedback — while deliberately inserting one full recovery session.
suggested_sessions: 3 km Zone 3 tempo (HR 145–155 bpm, cadence ≥152 spm)
4 km mixed-effort run: 2 km Z2 (HR 125–135 bpm), 1 km Z3 (HR 145–155 bpm), 1 km Z2
